name: Hirobius Design System
description: Three-tier design token system for Adrian Milsap's portfolio site. Tokens flow primitive → semantic → component. All visual decisions trace back to hirobius.tokens.json.

architecture.aliasRule: Semantic, component, and role tokens must alias an upstream token, except computed oklch() color values for derived tints.
architecture.darkMode: [data-theme="dark"] on <html>. Semantic tokens auto-switch via CSS var chain — no JS re-renders.
architecture.consumeTypography: hds.typeStyles.h1 (inline style object) — never Tailwind font classes

tokens.name: heading1
tokens.role: Primary section headings and h1 text

tokens.name: heading2
tokens.role: Secondary section headings and h2 text

tokens.name: heading3
tokens.role: Component headings, card headings, and h3 text

tokens.name: body
tokens.role: Default long-form prose and body copy

tokens.name: ui
tokens.role: Standard interface text, nav labels, inputs, and controls

tokens.name: caption
tokens.role: Active functional role for helper text, error metadata, secondary metadata, and form annotations

tokens.name: technical
tokens.role: Monospace token keys, code snippets, keyboard shortcuts, hashes, and aligned data readouts

tokens.name: badge
tokens.role: Status badges and compact tags only

phases.label: Foundations
phases.description: Token architecture, CSS pipeline, accessibility, and primitive scales.

phases.label: Components
phases.description: Accessible React UI built from foundations and mapped toward Figma parity.

criteria.label: Prop tables and API doc coverage

phases.label: Architecture
phases.description: System delivery, Figma sync, automation, quality gates, and operational hygiene.

breakingChangePolicy: Version increments on any prop rename, token path rename, or removed component. Additions are non-breaking.

CaseStudyLayout.description: CaseStudyLayout — macro-layout skeleton for portfolio case study pages. Invisible structure only. No Surface, no decorative chrome. Slot anatomy: heroSlot → full-width (maxWidth="max") — hero image + headline introSlot → prose-width (maxWidth="content") — narrative, Brief/Problem/Solution metricsSlot → full-width (maxWidth="max") — KPI or summary cards (optional) contentSlot → full-width (maxWidth="max") — chapters, galleries, learnings

a11yRules.rule: Root element is <main> — only one CaseStudyLayout per page

Alert.description: Alert - compact feedback surface with contextual severity.

a11yRules.rule: role=alert is set by the component — do not override

CinematicLink.description: CinematicLink - cinematic editorial link treatment for portfolio surfaces.

CodeBlock.description: CodeBlock - code display with copy button and optional collapsible toggle.

Container.description:
Container — semantic width-constrained layout. Centers content horizontally and applies semantic max-width constraints. - maxWidth options: 'content' (760px prose) | 'max' (1200px full layout) - No arbitrary pixel values allowed Usage:
...

Disclosure.description: Disclosure - compact disclosure surface for optional explanatory content.

Divider.description: Divider — semantic separator between content regions.

DocLinkCard.description: DocLinkCard - navigation card for editorial and documentation cross-links.

a11yRules.rule: Card link must have descriptive accessible name (not just the URL)
Focus ring visible on keyboard navigation

ErrorPattern.description: ErrorPattern - governed recovery surface for routed application errors.

a11yRules.rule: Headline renders as <h1> via Text variant=display — only one ErrorPattern per page

FoundationSwatch.description: FoundationSwatch - governed foundation specimen for color and semantic role previews.

Grid.description: Grid — responsive grid composition primitive. Enforces semantic gap and column values. No arbitrary CSS grid. - layout='fixed': responsive base (collapses via CSS at tablet/mobile). `columns` sets desktop count (default 12). Tablet/mobile clamp to min(8, cols) and min(4, cols) respectively. - layout='auto-fit': responsive card wrapping via auto-fit. - subgrid=true: sets gridTemplateColumns:'subgrid' for nested alignment. Usage (default responsive 12-col): <Grid> <Grid.Item colSpan={6}>…</Grid.Item> <Grid.Item colSpan={6}>…</Grid.Item> </Grid> Usage (responsive auto-fit): <Grid layout="auto-fit"> <Card /> <Card /> </Grid>

Icon.description: Icon — semantic icon wrapper for Lucide icons.

a11yRules.rule: Must have aria-label when used without adjacent text

IconButton.description: IconButton ” icon-only action trigger built on the shared Button primitive.

a11yRules.rule: Must have aria-label (icon-only buttons have no visible text)
Focus ring visible in all interactive states (uses hds-focus class)

InfoPage.description: InfoPage - branded profile surface used for the portfolio landing page.

a11yRules.rule: Author name renders as <h1> via Text variant=heading3 — only one InfoPage per page
Profile image must have a non-empty alt describing the subject

InlineCode.description: InlineCode — inline code chip for token paths, file paths, and code-adjacent prose.

InlineLink.description: InlineLink ” inline navigation and external-link primitive for body copy.

a11yRules.rule: Link text must be descriptive — avoid "click here" or "read more"
External links must signal new-tab behavior via aria-label or visually hidden text

Input.description: Input — text field primitive (shadcn baseline). shadcn-baseline implementation (8s-5): cva-driven sizing/state composed against role-token Tailwind utilities (bg-background / border-input / focus-visible:ring-ring / placeholder:text-muted-foreground / etc.) wired by 8e-2 + 8s-1. Border swaps to border-destructive on error and the focus ring follows. Theme is handled entirely by CSS custom properties. The label/helper/error wrapper is deliberately retained on this primitive so existing call sites keep working unchanged. A future 8t pass may extract those slots into an HdsField pattern (see orchestration agentNote). Variants: size — sm (32px) / md (40px, default) / lg (48px) textStyle — body (default) / mono Derived state (data-state): default | focus | filled | error | disabled | loading loading swaps the trailing area for a spinner and sets aria-busy; disabled is implied. demoState (frozen, via DemoStateContext) is honored so matrix cells still freeze visually.

a11yRules.rule: Must have associated label via label prop or aria-labelledby

NavGroup.description: NavGroup - labeled navigation group for stacks of nav items.

a11yRules.rule: Navigation group must have accessible label (aria-label on the nav element)
Expanded/collapsed state communicated via aria-expanded

NavItem.description: NavItem - navigation row primitive for sidebars, table of contents, and list navigation.

a11yRules.rule: Active item must be communicated via aria-current="page" or aria-selected
Focus ring visible in all interactive states (uses hds-focus class)

SegmentedControl.description: SegmentedControl " segmented selection input for compact mutually-exclusive choices.

a11yRules.rule: Must have accessible group label (aria-label or aria-labelledby on the container)
Selected option must be communicated via aria-pressed or aria-selected

Sketch.description: Sketch — shared shell for generative canvases and WebGL sketches.

StepperField.description: StepperField ” numeric input with decrement/increment controls. Classification baseline: Material Design, Chakra UI, and Ant Design treat steppers as numeric input controls rather than page-specific controls. This lives in shared components so sketches and docs consume the same primitive.

a11yRules.rule: Must have associated label via label prop or aria-labelledby
Decrement/increment buttons must have aria-label

Table.description: Table - structured data table primitive for documentation and compact UI matrices.

a11yRules.rule: Column headers must use <th> with scope="col", row headers with scope="row"
Table must have caption or aria-label describing its contents

Tag.description: Tag — interactive filter and category chip. The outer <button> carries the accessible 44px hit target directly via the size token while the inner pill keeps the visible surface compact. Colors (background, border-color, color) are owned by .hds-tag-btn / .hds-tag-pill in theme.css so :hover transitions work — inline styles beat :hover selectors and would silently break the effect. Active state: brand fill via [data-active="true"] in theme.css. Hover states: inactive → subtle tint + brand border; active → brand-hover fill.

a11yRules.rule: When used as interactive chip (onClick), must have role="button" and keyboard activation
When used as status indicator (no onClick), role should be "status" or omitted

Token.description: Token - reflective token specimen for unified node-based token views.

Surface.description: Surface — governed inset surface primitive. Enforces the "Surface Inset" rule: backgrounds MUST have internal padding. - Default padding: px24 (cards) | px16 (items) - Default border: none (elevation model — use shadow for lift) - Fixed radius: var(--component-card-radius) - shadow prop: applies a two-layer elevation shadow for card lift Usage: <Surface>Card content</Surface> <Surface padding="item">Compact item</Surface> <Surface shadow>Lifted card with elevation shadow.</Surface>

Card.description: Card — surface container with slot anatomy (12d-card-anatomy). Slot anatomy (DESIGN.md §"Card Anatomy"). Status, progress, and metadata are layout-bearing — they live in slots, never inline next to prose. <Card tone="accent"> <Card.Header metadata={<Badge tone="warning">in-progress</Badge>}> <Card.Title>Discovery phase</Card.Title> <Card.Description>Stakeholder interviews and audit prep.</Card.Description> </Card.Header> <Card.Progress value={42} max={100} label="5 / 12 tasks" /> <Card.Metric label="Retainer" value="$3,500" sub="Active" /> <Card.Body>…</Card.Body> <Card.Footer>…</Card.Footer> </Card> Slot reservations: - Header: title (h3) + optional description (caption) + optional metadata zone (right-aligned, holds <Badge> / <Tag> for status, never raw spans). - Progress: full-width 4px bar in a reserved 16px vertical rail. Owns its spacing — never crowds adjacent prose. - Metric: single label-uppercase + big value (h2) + optional sub-line. Reserved vertical block. Use multiple side-by-side via flex/grid container. - Body: prose, lists, structured content. NO inline status, progress, or thin colored bars. Group sections via separate <Card.Body> blocks. - Footer: actions, secondary metadata, timestamps. Tone prop: 'default' (neutral border) | 'accent' (border-accent + raised bg) for highlighted variants like "recommended package", or feedback tones for status-driven cards. Tone replaces hand-rolled `pkgHighlight`-style overrides. Legacy props (padding / gap / noPadding / as / style) are retained. Existing callers that pass raw children continue to work; new callers should prefer compound parts + `padding="none"`. Depth comes from semantic.color.border.default (role.border) at rest; elevation tokens are not bound by default — pass `className="shadow-..."` for a hover/floating treatment on interactive cards.

a11yRules.rule: componentName must match a manifest entry — drives accessible heading text
Tab panels are announced via segmented control aria-label

ComponentInstanceMatrix.description: ComponentInstanceMatrix — responsive specimen matrix for variant and state parity.

SpecimenBlock.description: SpecimenBlock — shared component-doc specimen block with preview, variant deck, and optional matrix.

ActivityFeed.description: ActivityFeed — chronological event log using whitespace as the sole separator between items. Use for timeline views and system audit trails; reach for Table when columnar comparison matters. Enforces 48px gap (space.12) between events and semantic typography roles throughout.

HeadingStack.description: HeadingStack — enforced vertical rhythm for heading + subheading pairs. Automatically handles gap and secondary color, preventing manual stacking. - Heading level: 'heading1' | 'heading2' | 'heading3' - Gap: px4 (tight) | px8 (default) - Subheading color: always var(--semantic-color-content-secondary) GUARDRAIL: Never manually stack headings using Stack. Always use HeadingStack. Usage: <HeadingStack level="heading1" heading="Main Title" subheading="Subtitle text" /> <HeadingStack level="heading2" heading="Section" subheading="Description" gap="px4" />

HdsSystemDocLayout.description: HdsSystemDocLayout — three-column doc layout with sidebar, nav, and content.

a11yRules.rule: Content sections must register with HdsTocContext to populate the on-this-page TOC

Text.description: Text — polymorphic typography primitive spanning the full HDS type ramp.

Dialog.description: Dialog — modal dialog (shadcn baseline, compound parts). shadcn-baseline implementation (8s-7): Radix Dialog primitive (@radix-ui/react-dialog) themed with role tokens. Provides focus trap, scroll lock, ESC-to-close, backdrop scrim, and portal mounting out of the box. <Dialog> <Dialog.Trigger asChild> <Button>Open</Button> </Dialog.Trigger> <Dialog.Content> <Dialog.Header> <Dialog.Title>Confirm</Dialog.Title> <Dialog.Description>Are you sure?</Dialog.Description> </Dialog.Header> <Dialog.Footer> <Dialog.Close asChild> <Button variant="secondary">Cancel</Button> </Dialog.Close> </Dialog.Footer> </Dialog.Content> </Dialog> Surface uses role.popover (semantic.color.surface.overlay) + shadow-overlay (semantic.shadow.overlay from 8e-1). The scrim is a foreground/80 wash so it picks up the theme without a hardcoded black. The close affordance is rendered as an absolutely-positioned X inside Content; pass `hideClose` to opt out for fully-custom layouts.

a11yRules.rule: Must have an accessible name via Dialog.Title or aria-label
Description should be associated via Dialog.Description when present

StackedCardRail.description: StackedCardRail — horizontally-scrolling stacked card carousel. Scroll mechanism: vertical scroll on an absolutely-positioned inner container is mapped to horizontal scroll on a sticky inner strip via a single scroll event listener. Cards use CSS scroll-driven animation (animation-timeline: view(x …)) for the stack/unstack effect where the browser supports it; a plain horizontal rail is the graceful fallback. Browser compat note: - animation-timeline: view() — Chrome 115+, Firefox 110+ (behind flag until v114), Safari 18+ (TP only as of 2025). The @supports guard ensures the stacking animation is only applied when the feature is available; all other browsers get a clean scrollable row. - :has() selector (neighbour shift on hover) — Chrome 105+, FF 121+, Safari 15.4+. Gated with @supports selector(:has(+ *)).

a11yRules.rule: Must have accessible name via label prop or aria-label
Focus ring visible in all interactive states (uses hds-focus class)

Callout.description: Tone-driven side-rule callout for status, quotes, hypotheses.

a11yRules.rule: Callout is decorative; tone is signaled visually — pair with semantic role when meaning matters

EmptyState.description:
EmptyState — consistent no-data placeholder for lists, grids, and sections. communicate "no data yet" with consistent muted typography instead of agents inventing one-off `

No items.

` lines. use for full-page errors (use ErrorPage) or for loading states (use a skeleton). Do NOT wrap in Surface or Card — the empty state is a typographic message, not a chrome-bearing container.

Field.description: Caption label paired with a value — used in metadata grids and read-only forms.

a11yRules.rule: Label must describe the value; pair with aria-describedby when value is non-trivial

Page.description: Page — standard page shell. Wraps page content in Container (width constraint) and applies the canonical vertical padding so content breathes against the viewport edges on scroll. Use as the outermost element of any new page so vertical rhythm is consistent across the system without per-page reinvention. Footer: every page mounted under HDSLayout (the standard route shell) gets a shared footer rendered after this component's content, with bottom space below the footer owned by the layout shell. Page intentionally does not render its own footer — the shell is the single source of truth. Use Container directly only for full-bleed surfaces (sketches, hero sections, embedded canvases) where the page owns its own vertical padding. Usage: <Page> <Stack direction="column" gap="spacious">...</Stack> </Page> <Page maxWidth="content" paddingY="compact"> <article>...</article> </Page>

Stat.description: Headline metric — large value, uppercase caption label, optional sub-line.

a11yRules.rule: Provide an accessible name for the stat when value is symbolic (—, ✓, etc.)

StatusListItem.description: Status dot + title row with optional muted notes and trailing slot (e.g. badge).

a11yRules.rule: Status dot is decorative (aria-hidden); convey status meaning via title or trailing badge

StatusTile.description: Block-layout sibling of StatusListItem. Renders a raised surface tile with title, optional muted notes, and a trailing slot for a badge or affordance. Status colour belongs in the trailing slot, never as a left rule on the tile — every tile should look the same so feedback signals stay reserved for genuinely interrupting states.

TileGrid.description: Auto-fill responsive grid for status/micro tiles. Wraps tile children in a one-line grid declaration so pages don't repeat the auto-fill minmax recipe.

Badge.description: Compact metadata/status chip. Tone is the only styling input.

AssetImg.description: AssetImg - responsive asset frame with fallback placeholder handling. Contexts: default, lightbox, and detail.

a11yRules.rule: Must have alt text (decorative images use alt="")
Caption or description should be associated via aria-describedby when present

Tabs.description: Tabs — Radix-backed underline tabs (Root, List, Trigger, Content). Underline pattern math: - TabsList carries `border-b border-border/40` (the divider, half-strength so it anchors the strip without competing with content). - TabsTrigger has `border-b-2 border-transparent -mb-px` at rest. The `-mb-px` offsets the trigger DOWN by 1px so the trigger sits on top of the list divider; the active 2px bottom border visually REPLACES that 1px segment instead of stacking on it. - Active state pairs the underline with a soft `bg-accent/5` tint that extends down into TabsContent — the active tab visually "claims" its content panel (folder-tab affordance, modern-soft). Overflow: TabsList is horizontally scrollable (`overflow-x-auto whitespace-nowrap`) for cases like /ops/atlas with 8 tabs at narrow viewports. Scrollbar chrome is hidden cross-browser (-webkit / firefox / IE).

TabsContent.description: Tabs — Radix-backed underline tabs (Root, List, Trigger, Content). Underline pattern math: - TabsList carries `border-b border-border/40` (the divider, half-strength so it anchors the strip without competing with content). - TabsTrigger has `border-b-2 border-transparent -mb-px` at rest. The `-mb-px` offsets the trigger DOWN by 1px so the trigger sits on top of the list divider; the active 2px bottom border visually REPLACES that 1px segment instead of stacking on it. - Active state pairs the underline with a soft `bg-accent/5` tint that extends down into TabsContent — the active tab visually "claims" its content panel (folder-tab affordance, modern-soft). Overflow: TabsList is horizontally scrollable (`overflow-x-auto whitespace-nowrap`) for cases like /ops/atlas with 8 tabs at narrow viewports. Scrollbar chrome is hidden cross-browser (-webkit / firefox / IE).

TabsList.description: Tabs — Radix-backed underline tabs (Root, List, Trigger, Content). Underline pattern math: - TabsList carries `border-b border-border/40` (the divider, half-strength so it anchors the strip without competing with content). - TabsTrigger has `border-b-2 border-transparent -mb-px` at rest. The `-mb-px` offsets the trigger DOWN by 1px so the trigger sits on top of the list divider; the active 2px bottom border visually REPLACES that 1px segment instead of stacking on it. - Active state pairs the underline with a soft `bg-accent/5` tint that extends down into TabsContent — the active tab visually "claims" its content panel (folder-tab affordance, modern-soft). Overflow: TabsList is horizontally scrollable (`overflow-x-auto whitespace-nowrap`) for cases like /ops/atlas with 8 tabs at narrow viewports. Scrollbar chrome is hidden cross-browser (-webkit / firefox / IE).

TabsTrigger.description: Tabs — Radix-backed underline tabs (Root, List, Trigger, Content). Underline pattern math: - TabsList carries `border-b border-border/40` (the divider, half-strength so it anchors the strip without competing with content). - TabsTrigger has `border-b-2 border-transparent -mb-px` at rest. The `-mb-px` offsets the trigger DOWN by 1px so the trigger sits on top of the list divider; the active 2px bottom border visually REPLACES that 1px segment instead of stacking on it. - Active state pairs the underline with a soft `bg-accent/5` tint that extends down into TabsContent — the active tab visually "claims" its content panel (folder-tab affordance, modern-soft). Overflow: TabsList is horizontally scrollable (`overflow-x-auto whitespace-nowrap`) for cases like /ops/atlas with 8 tabs at narrow viewports. Scrollbar chrome is hidden cross-browser (-webkit / firefox / IE).

HdsRadio.description: HdsRadio — radio button with animated selection indicator.

HdsSlider.description: HdsSlider — range slider with label and value display.

HdsToggle.description: HdsToggle — boolean on/off toggle with animated thumb.

Avatar.description: Circular avatar. Renders the image when available, else initials from `alt`.

Progress.description: Linear progress. Determinate when `value` is a number, else indeterminate.

Skeleton.description: Decorative loading placeholder. Marked aria-hidden — announce the loading state on the surrounding region (e.g. aria-busy) rather than per skeleton.

Spinner.description: Indeterminate spinner. Inherits the surrounding text color via currentColor.

Breadcrumb.description: Breadcrumb trail. Links route through the HDS router seam (plain anchors with no router, client-side nav when a router adapter is provided). The final item is marked aria-current="page".

Pagination.description: Paged navigation with first/last anchors, sibling pages, and ellipses.

HdsCheckbox.description: HdsCheckbox — custom-drawn checkbox with check / indeterminate glyph.

HdsPhosphor.description: Compiler-recognized icon alias stub for JSX normalization.

HdsFrame.description: Compiler-internal frame primitive stub for generated JSX validation.

HdsHeading.description: Compiler-recognized heading primitive stub for generated JSX validation.

HdsLabel.description: Compiler-recognized label primitive stub for generated JSX validation.

HdsCaption.description: Compiler-recognized caption primitive stub for generated JSX validation.

HdsChip.description: Compiler-recognized chip instance stub for generated JSX validation.

HdsAvatar.description: Compiler-recognized avatar instance stub for generated JSX validation.

HdsPhosphor.description: Compiler-recognized icon alias stub for JSX normalization.

HdsFrame.description: Layout primitive — vertical or horizontal flex container on the 8px grid. Padding and gap from {0,8,16,24,32,48,64}; surfaces use semantic.color.surface.* tokens, never raw hex. Structural frames are rectilinear (no border-radius); only Card-wrapping frames pick up semantic.radius.card.

HdsHeading.description: Semantic heading wrapper. Maps level={1,2,3} to a typography token AND the matching h-tag (h1/h2/h3). Never skip levels (h1 → h3 without h2). Headings are font-light (display, h1) or font-normal (h2, h3) — never bold. Use Text for non-heading display copy.

HdsLabel.description: Form-control label primitive. Uses semantic.typography.caption (uppercase, tracked, font-medium). Color is semantic.color.content.secondary by default; the required-asterisk takes semantic.color.feedback.danger. Always pairs htmlFor with the control id; never used for non-label microcopy.

HdsCaption.description: Compiler-recognized caption primitive stub for generated JSX validation.

HdsChip.description: Compiler-recognized chip instance stub for generated JSX validation.

HdsAvatar.description: User identity glyph. Square or circle, sized on the 8px grid (24/32/40/48/64). Initial-letter fallback uses semantic.typography.small (font-medium, never bold). Image variant is object-cover, never object-fill. Radius is semantic.radius.action for square or semantic.radius.pill for circle.

AutoPreviewSpecimen.description: /** @internal @doc-exempt: demo-preview-helper — not part of @hirobius/design-system public API.

VariantPreviewDeck.description: /** @internal @doc-exempt: demo-preview-helper — not part of @hirobius/design-system public API.

HistoryCard.description: HistoryCard — compact commit-history card for repo and systems-log surfaces. Padding on the 8px grid; commit hash uses semantic.typography.mono (mono); secondary metadata via semantic.color.content.tertiary (opacity, never a different hue). No shadow — depth comes from semantic.color.border.default.

ComponentPreview.description: ComponentPreview - framed preview harness for utility and lab specimens.

AnimatedLabel.description: AnimatedLabel — animated text slot for use inside interactive surfaces. Renders the shared CinematicLink text treatment (slide-up reveal + underline draw) as a composable span, not a standalone anchor. Designed to slot inside HdsButton asChild or any other interactive surface that owns the hover group. Usage: add className="group" to the parent HdsButton (or any ancestor), then wrap the label text in <AnimatedLabel>. The group-hover classes activate on CSS :hover of the parent group element — no JS hover state needed. level. The parent surface (button, anchor, etc.) owns semantic role, focus, and pointer events. AnimatedLabel only owns the visual animation layer.

ControlsPanel.description: shared shell composition documented alongside the HDS doc utilities.

ControlsSection.description: shared shell composition documented alongside the HDS doc utilities.

HdsMobileTopBar.description: shell-only extraction for HDS navigation chrome, not a consumer-facing reusable HDS component.

PreviewFrame.description: PreviewFrame - documentation preview shell for storefront component demos.

HdsSidebarUtilityButton.description: shell-only extraction for HDS navigation chrome, not a consumer-facing reusable HDS component.

NotFoundPattern.description: NotFoundPattern - governed not-found recovery pattern for unroutable pages.

HdsDocPrimitives.description: Use HdsDocPrimitives as the shared documentation primitive module that powers HDS reflection.

SideNav.description: SideNav - sidebar navigation row primitive. Two levels: root — top-level section link. Full-width row using the shared nav label type style. nested — child page link. Keeps the same full-width hit area and label type treatment, with indentation handled via inner padding only. No indicator bar (reserved for HdsTocNav). Idle text = content-secondary → primary on hover/active. Accent surface fill on active state.

HdsLegacyTokenGovernancePanel.description: LegacyTokenDetail - lab-facing token inspection panels used to maintain legacy token views.

LegacyTokenList.description: LegacyTokenList - legacy token grouping list retained for lab-side maintenance workflows. architecture per TASKS.md "Stale / Superseded" → "Live token inspector UI". Pruning deferred until /hds/tokens is retired or rewritten — see docs/archive/work/2026-05-01-token-explorer-prune-deferred.md.

TokenCollectionList.description: TokenCollectionList - grouped token inventory list for lab and explorer maintenance. architecture per TASKS.md "Stale / Superseded" → "Live token inspector UI". Pruning deferred until /hds/tokens is retired or rewritten — see docs/archive/work/2026-05-01-token-explorer-prune-deferred.md.

TokenDetail.description: TokenDetail - canonical token detail inspector for the live token explorer and lab tooling.

TokenList.description: TokenList - searchable token rail for interactive lab-side token inspection.

HdsFoundationSection.description: HdsDocPrimitives â€” shared doc-page building blocks. Previously embedded in HDSLayout.tsx. Extracted so page files can import from a single, focused module without pulling in the entire shell. Import pattern (from a sibling hds/ page): import { DocPageHeader, DocSection, useIsMobile } from './HdsDocPrimitives'; Import pattern (from hds/components/ sub-page): import { DocPageHeader, DocSection, ComponentBlock } from '../HdsDocPrimitives';

ErrorBoundary.description: ErrorBoundary — slot-scoped recovery surface for runtime render failures.

ApiReference.description: ApiReference — collapsed <details> rendering of a component's public API. Returns `null` when: - The component is missing from the manifest (nothing to render). - The component is a template and `forceForTemplate` is not set. - The component has no props AND no slots (genuinely empty surface).

CommandPalette.description: Mountable trigger + dialog. Drop into header slot; component manages its own open state and Cmd-K / Ctrl-K global keybinding.

DocPageHeader.description: DocPageHeader — standardized header zone mounted at the top of every primitive / pattern / template doc page. Projected from the live HDS manifest; the unit `9d-9` batch refactor wires this component into the doc routes — this file only CREATES the surface. 9d-4 contract (from `docs/ai/orchestration.json`): - H1: component name - Lede: single-line summary derived from `componentSpec.description` (truncated at the first newline / sentence break for the header zone; the full description still flows into the doc body downstream). - Status badge: Stable / Beta — derived from a new optional `componentSpec.stability` field (additive, schema 9d-4). When absent, the default falls back to the unit-spec rule: primitives → 'stable'; patterns and templates → 'beta' when they lack `slots[]` or carry propConstraints warnings, otherwise 'stable'. - Tier chip: primitive / pattern / template / utility (manifest field). - Source link: GitHub blob URL projected from `componentSpec.filePath` against the repo's origin remote (hirobius/adrian-milsap, branch `main` for canonical permalinks). - Figma link: rendered when `componentSpec.figmaUrl` is non-null. Surface rules (component-zone CLAUDE.md): - Heavy prop restriction — one slot, no business logic. - Theming via CSS variables / role tokens only (no inline hex). - Composes Stack rather than re-implementing layout primitives. Anti-goals: - Not a doc-shell — `DocShell` already owns the wordmark, search, theme-toggle, and rail nav. This component lives INSIDE the content column, not the header bar. - Does not render badges as decorative stickers next to prose; they sit in a dedicated metadata row below the H1, per CLAUDE.md.

ThemeToggle.description: ThemeToggle — System / Light / Dark theme dropdown for the doc shell. 9d-3 baseline: a header-slot dropdown with three options: - System — follow `prefers-color-scheme`, react live to OS changes. - Light — force light mode. - Dark — force dark mode. Persistence: - Stored in localStorage under `hds-theme-mode` ('system' | 'light' | 'dark'). - Mirrored to the legacy `hds-theme` key ('light' | 'dark') so the existing ThemeContext binary toggle (if mounted) stays in sync without a hard dependency between the two. Application: - Sets `data-theme="light"` or `data-theme="dark"` on `<html>` so the 8e-2 `role.*` alias layer (theme-aware CSS vars) flips automatically. - Also toggles `<html>.dark` for parity with Tailwind's class-based dark variant (matching the convention already used in ThemeContext). Dependencies: - Zero new npm deps. Native button + click-outside listener. - SVG icons inlined (Sun / Moon / Monitor) — no icon-library dep added. Slot: - Renders into the `data-hds-slot="theme-toggle"` zone in DocShell.

VariantStrip.description: VariantStrip renders a horizontal family strip of component specimens with short labels. Designed for component docs to show all variants, sizes, or tones in a single scannable row.

PageFooter.description: PageFooter — minimal page footer rendered by the route shell. Currently houses a binary light/dark theme toggle on the left, with the footer owning its own bottom breathing room so the toggle never pins to the viewport edge. Add elements here as the page footer grows — keeping the shape in one place so every HDSLayout-mounted page gets a consistent footer surface without per-page rendering.

semantic.description: For micro-interactions and status changes. No deformation.
For teaching moments and significant UI entries. Includes physics-based squish.
For elements traveling long distances across the viewport.
For elements being removed from the DOM.
Main page background
Subtle elevated surface (cards, sections)
Deeper surface layer
Inverted background for contrast sections
Accent-colored background
Faint accent tint for hover states or highlights
Lowest-contrast border for resting hairline edges on flat surfaces.
1px border offset and hairline spacing.
2px vertical rhythm nudge and baseline adjustment.
4px cap-height and inline cluster spacing.
8px label↔input rhythm and closely coupled control spacing.
12px adjacent form fields and medium intra-component gaps.
24px default inset for cards, forms, panels, and grouped surfaces.
16px default HdsStack gap and inter-component spacing.
24px column gutters and sidebar gutter spacing.
24px column and region gutters (alias for layout.normal).
32px major block separation and inset inter-component gaps.
48px section break and whitespace separator between major content blocks.
80px vertical rhythm between page sections and major editorial blocks.
96px hero and landing-page inset padding.
128px maximum hero region padding for extra-large displays.
12px indent level for nested sidebar and TOC groups.
16px vertical spacing between items in sidebar and TOC stacks.
12px spacing between sidebar sections and grouped rail blocks.
20px outer padding for sidebar and TOC rails.
Max width for prose/content (760px). Use with HdsContainer for centered text blocks.
Max width for full-layout containers (1200px). Use with HdsContainer for page layouts.
Display headline. 72px / Clash Display 500 / leading-none.
Primary section headings (h1). 48px / Clash Display medium 500 / leading-tight.
Secondary section headings (h2). Clash Display medium, 30px, line-height 42px.
Component and card headers (h3). 20px / Clash Display medium 500 / leading-snug.
Body prose. Satoshi medium, 17px, line-height 28px, max 60ch measure.
UI text for nav, labels, captions. Satoshi medium, 15px, line-height 24px.
Eyebrow / kicker label. Satoshi medium, 13px, caps tracking, uppercase.
Monospace for code, token names, shortcuts. Geist Mono regular, 13px.
Semantic alias for leading-none (1). Display text and badge spans.
48px between H2 sections — generous section break for doc pages.
24px between paragraphs — comfortable prose rhythm for long-form doc content.
8px inline cluster spacing — gap between metadata badges, inline icons, doc-page chips.

Accent fill for disabled accent elements.

Modal/dialog elevation for full-screen overlays.
Flat surface (no shadow, hairline border) for embedded panels.
Resting card / panel elevation.
Hover, menu, and popover elevation.
Modal, dialog, and drawer elevation.
